original_abstract: Adapting large language models (LLMs) to specialized downstream tasks relies heavily on efficient fine-tuning techniques. However, implementing these methods across diverse model architectures often demands significant engineering effort. To address this, we introduce LLaMA-Factory, a unified framework that scales and simplifies the fine-tuning process by integrating a wide array of cutting-edge efficiency-driven algorithms. Through its intuitive web interface, LLaMA-Board, the framework offers a no-code solution for customizing over 100 distinct LLMs. Our empirical evaluations demonstrate that LLaMA-Factory maintains high performance in language modeling and text generation while significantly reducing computational overhead. Since its release, the project has gained substantial community traction, garnering over 25,000 stars and 3,000 forks on GitHub (https://github.com/hiyouga/LLaMA-Factory).
humanized_abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) for task-specific performance is a ubiquitous practice across the industry, and the speed and ease of doing so is vital for successful model deployment. Achieving this efficiency while supporting a wide variety of models is typically a substantial engineering task. We present LLaMA-Factory, a flexible framework for instantiating recent fine-tuning acceleration techniques while being highly scalable and easy to use. Via the accompanying no-code web interface LLaMA-Board, users can fine-tune to optimal performance any of the 100+ different LLMs we currently support. We measure the trade-offs of LLaMA-Factory between high-performance model behavior, overall training time, and overall memory consumption, finding that it is competitive to and often better than other accelerations approaches. Finally, we share our findings and open-source release of this project which has so far garnered more than 25,000 GitHub stars and 3,000 forks (https://github.com/hiyouga/LLaMA-Factory).
